hypothesis: In Si–X alloy anodes with theoretical capacity >1500 mAh/g, a Li-ion channel-radius vs. state-of-charge slope ≤0.45 Å per 100% SOC will predict ≥80% capacity retention and ≤35% electrode thickness swelling after 200 C/2 cycles in 1 M LiPF6 in EC:DEC (1:1) with 10% FEC, whereas alloys with slopes >0.45 Å per 100% SOC will fail at least one of these metrics.
